content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement (App/DealAlerts.entitlements: aps-environment=production) paired with promotional spam-push pattern: hourly repeating marketing notifications in App/NotificationScheduler.swift:11 (`timeInterval: 3600, repeats: true`, "Hot deal alert!") and metadata promising "alert you EVERY HOUR … unlimited notifications" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:3) may trigger review questions.
Pierre: Apple's guideline 4.5.3/4.5.4 prohibits using notifications for spam or marketing without explicit user consent and opt-out, and reviewers watch for promotional push at high frequency. Both the code and the store description advertise hourly, unlimited promotional alerts, which reads as notification abuse rather than user-initiated alerts. Consider making deal alerts opt-in per user preference, reducing frequency, and removing the "EVERY HOUR / unlimited notifications" language from the metadata.
